items.title: Colorado National Guard shifts to year-round wildfire posture amid record low snowpack
items.description:
Due to record-low snowpack and an anticipated surge of up to 8,000 wildfires, the Colorado Army National Guard is transitioning to a year-round firefighting mission.
In response, the Guard is conducting rigorous collaborative training and prioritizing heavy-lift aircraft to ensure rapid, long-term aerial defense alongside local and federal partners.

items.title: Lethal Kinetic Strike, May 27, 2026
items.description: On May 27, at the direction of the commander of U.S. Southern Command Gen. Francis L. Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations. Intelligence confirmed the vessel was transiting along known narco-trafficking routes in the Eastern Pacific and was engaged in narco-trafficking operations. Two male narco-terrorists were killed during this action. No U.S. military forces were harmed.

items.title: From Survivor to Leader: BJACH commander shares lessons in resilience during Mental Health Awareness Month

items.title: At mile 11, she wanted to quit. Marines made sure she didn’t.
items.description:
MARINE CORPS BASE QUANTICO, Va.—By the time Gaby Trujillo reached Hospital Hill, every step felt heavier than the last; her legs ached, arms heavy, dizziness set in, and with only two miles remaining in the Marine Corps Historic Half Marathon, the first-time half marathoner began wondering whether she could finish at all.

items.title: U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Waesche returns to Alameda after successful 113-day patrol in Bering Sea
items.description:
ALAMEDA, Calif. – The crew of U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Waesche (WMSL 751) returned to their homeport in Alameda Friday after concluding a 113-day patrol in the Bering Sea. The cutter traveled 18,685 nautical miles conducting a broad range of operations including maritime law enforcement, search and rescue and the integration of new capabilities, all while projecting U.S. presence in the high north environment.

items.title: MCRD San Diego Golf Company Graduation - 05/22/26
items.description: The new Marines of Golf Company, 2nd Recruit Training Battalion, graduated from Marine Corps Recruit Depot San Diego, May 22 2026. items.title: Duty in Japan: Halfway to Kyoto on the Nakasendō Trail
items.description: The Nakasendō (meaning ‘central mountain road’ in English), is a 331-mile (534km) route that was established during the Edo Period by the Tokugawa Shogunate to connect Tokyo and Kyoto. Much of the route has evolved with modern times, but sections of the trail are kept to its original condition. The route can still be traveled on foot today.
